perturbation.original_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. as royalty a sum equal to % of the net wholesale volume of the products covered by this Agreement by Diplomat and its affiliated, associated, or subsidiary companies. The term "net wholesale volume" shall mean gross sales to all customers; less returns, trade discounts and cash discounts; but no deduction shall be made for other discounts or uncollectible accounts. No costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement shall be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat.
perturbation.changed_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. as royalty a sum equal to % of the net wholesale volume of the products covered by this Agreement by Diplomat and its affiliated, associated, or subsidiary companies. The term "net wholesale volume" shall mean gross sales to all customers; less returns. Costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement can be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat.
perturbation.explanation: Here the definition of 'net wholesale volume' is changed. Initially, no costs were deductible from the royalty payable, but the modified definition now allows for the deduction of manufacturing, sales, and other costs. This introduces an in-text contradiction to this section, as it conflicts with the following statement in the original text: 'No costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement shall be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Diplomat agrees to pay KI, Inc. as royalty a sum equal to % of the net wholesale volume of the products covered by this Agreement by Diplomat and its affiliated, associated, or subsidiary companies. The term "net wholesale volume" shall mean gross sales to all customers; less returns. Costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement can be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat. No costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement shall be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'net wholesale volume' by allowing the deduction of costs, which directly contradicts the statement in the contradicted text that 'No costs incurred in the manufacture, sale, distribution, or exploitation of the products covered by this Agreement shall be deducted from any royalty payable by Diplomat.' This creates a clear and direct conflict within the contract, making it an in-text contradiction. The original definition does not allow for any deductions, and the modified version does.

perturbation.original_text: KI will make one (1) personal appearance per License Year during the period of this Agreement on behalf of Diplomat at the Vision Expo in New York, New York, subject to the terms of this paragraph. Such appearance shall be for the purpose of signing autographs, shall last for a period of up to three (3) hours, and shall be subject to KI's schedule and availability. Diplomat, at its own expense, shall provide KI with a hair and make-up assistant of KI's choosing for each personal or media appearance required under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: KI may make personal appearances as she sees fit. No appearance shall be scheduled unless KI deems it necessary. Diplomat shall not be required to provide KI with a hair and make-up assistant of KI's choosing for any appearance required under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that 'KI will make one personal appearance,' implying an obligation. The changed text states 'KI may make personal appearances as she sees fit,' and 'no appearance shall be scheduled unless KI deems it necessary,' making it discretionary. Additionally, the obligation for Diplomat to provide a hair and makeup assistant is removed. This creates an in-text contradiction in this very section, directly contradicting the original intent and requirements for KI's appearances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: KI may make personal appearances as she sees fit. No appearance shall be scheduled unless KI deems it necessary. Diplomat shall not be required to provide KI with a hair and make-up assistant of KI's choosing for any appearance required under this Agreement. Such appearance shall be for the purpose of signing autographs, shall last for a period of up to three (3) hours, and shall be subject to KI's schedule and availability. Diplomat, at its own expense, shall provide KI with a hair and make-up assistant of KI's choosing for each personal or media appearance required under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated 'KI will make one personal appearance,' an obligation. The changed text states 'KI may make personal appearances as she sees fit,' making it discretionary. The changed text removes the requirement for Diplomat to provide a hair and makeup assistant, directly contradicting the original agreement. The contradiction is in the same section and is very explicit, causing a clear conflict between the two stipulations, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, creating an in-text contradiction by altering the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Diplomat agrees that the products covered by this Agreement shall be of such style, appearance and quality as to be adequate and suited to their exploitation to the best advantage, protection and enhancement of KI's name and the good will pertaining to such name. Diplomat further agrees that (a) such products will be manufactured, sold and distributed in accordance with all applicable Federal, State and local laws, (b) that the policy of sale, distribution, and/or exploitation by Diplomat shall be to the best advantage of KI, Inc. and KI, and (c) that the latter policy shall in no manner reflect adversely upon the good name of KI and KI, Inc.
perturbation.changed_text: Diplomat agrees that the products covered by this Agreement may or may not conform to a certain style, appearance and quality. Diplomat does not have to agree that the products will be manufactured, sold and distributed in accordance with all applicable Federal, State and local laws. The only requirement is that Diplomat ensures that (c) that the latter policy shall in no manner reflect adversely upon the good name of KI and KI, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates a certain standard for style, appearance, quality and adherence to laws. The modified text removes these requirements. While the last part of the original text and change text is being kept, the requirements of the original text in where the products have to conform to a certain style, appearance and quality as well that they have to be manufactured, sold and distributed in accordance with all applicable Federal, State and local laws, creates an in-text contradiction with the new change text, in where now, Diplomat agrees that the products covered by this Agreement may or may not conform to a certain style, appearance and quality as well that they aren't binded to manufacture, sell or distribute the product in accordance to laws.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Diplomat agrees that the products covered by this Agreement may or may not conform to a certain style, appearance and quality. Diplomat does not have to agree that the products will be manufactured, sold and distributed in accordance with all applicable Federal, State and local laws. The only requirement is that Diplomat ensures that (c) that the latter policy shall in no manner reflect adversely upon the good name of KI and KI, Inc. Diplomat agrees that the products covered by this Agreement shall be of such style, appearance and quality as to be adequate and suited to their exploitation to the best advantage, protection and enhancement of KI's name and the good will pertaining to such name. Diplomat further agrees that (a) such products will be manufactured, sold and distributed in accordance with all applicable Federal, State and local laws, (b) that the policy of sale, distribution, and/or exploitation by Diplomat shall be to the best advantage of KI, Inc. and KI, and (c) that the latter policy shall in no manner reflect adversely upon the good name of KI and KI, Inc.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the products covered by the agreement may or may not conform to a certain style, appearance, and quality, and that Diplomat does not have to agree that the products will be manufactured, sold, and distributed in accordance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the products covered by the Agreement *shall* be of a certain style, appearance and quality and *shall* be manufactured, sold and distributed in accordance with all applicable laws. This creates a clear conflict regarding the standards and legal compliance required of Diplomat.
